quick_facts.label: Current official naming
quick_facts.value: The exam commonly called “IBPS Clerk” was titled the Common Recruitment Process for Customer Service Associates (CRP-CSA-XV) in the most recent official clerical-cadre notification; it recruited for clerical-cadre Customer Service Associate posts in participating public-sector banks.

quick_facts.label: Conducting body and purpose
quick_facts.value: IBPS is an autonomous selection body. Its CRP programme conducts recruitment processes for participating public-sector banks, including Customer Service Associates (the current clerical-cadre designation).

quick_facts.label: Latest process status
quick_facts.value: As reviewed on 2026-07-18, the official Clerical Cadre XV page listed provisional-allotment and main-examination-result updates dated 2026-07-17. A fresh CSA/clerical notification and its dates should be treated as unannounced unless published by IBPS.

quick_facts.label: Most recent baseline eligibility
quick_facts.value: For CRP-CSA-XV, candidates needed a graduation degree in any discipline, computer working knowledge, and proficiency in the official language of the applied-for State/UT. The age band was 20–28 years as on 2025-08-01, with notified relaxations for eligible categories.

quick_facts.label: Selection format
quick_facts.value: CRP-CSA-XV used two online objective-test stages: Preliminary and Main. The preliminary test had English Language, Numerical Ability, and Reasoning Ability; the main test had General/Financial Awareness, General English, Reasoning Ability & Computer Aptitude, and Quantitative Aptitude. Wrong answers attracted a one-fourth-mark penalty in the cited cycle.

quick_facts.label: Outcome
quick_facts.value: IBPS conducts the process and makes provisional allotments; appointment is made by the allotted participating bank, which remains responsible for final employment decisions and eligibility verification.

sections.heading: What IBPS Clerk is now called and what it is for
paragraphs: “IBPS Clerk” is the widely used legacy name for IBPS’s clerical-cadre bank recruitment process. In the latest official cycle, IBPS used the title “Customer Service Associate” (CSA): CRP-CSA-XV invited candidates seeking clerical-cadre roles in participating public-sector banks for 2026–27 vacancies. The process is a common recruitment route rather than a job with IBPS itself.

IBPS describes itself as an autonomous body that supports organisations’ personnel selection. Under its flagship CRP programme, it runs selection processes for participating public-sector banks; candidates should rely on the specific cycle’s notification because participating banks, vacancy totals, State/UT vacancies, conditions, and schedule can change.
bullets: Use the official IBPS CRP-CSA/clerical-cadre page—not coaching-site calendars—as the authoritative source for a new application window.
The node title “IBPS Clerk” remains useful for search and recognition, but applicants should also search for “CRP-CSA” and “Customer Service Associate.”

sections.heading: Eligibility: use the next notification as the controlling document
paragraphs: Eligibility is cycle-specific. IBPS explicitly directs candidates to the relevant notification for age and educational requirements. The following is therefore a dated baseline from CRP-CSA-XV, not a guarantee for the next cycle.

For CRP-CSA-XV, the stated age requirement was 20 to 28 years as on 2025-08-01 (birth dates from 1997-08-02 through 2005-08-01, inclusive). The notice provided upper-age relaxations for eligible categories, including SC/ST, OBC (non-creamy layer), persons with benchmark disabilities, ex-servicemen/disabled ex-servicemen, and specified women candidates.

The same cycle required a graduation degree in any discipline from a Government-recognised university or an equivalent qualification. It also required operating and working knowledge of computer systems and proficiency—reading, writing, speaking, and understanding—in the official language of the State/UT applied for. Applicants should confirm documentary requirements, category rules, nationality/citizenship conditions, and local-language requirements in the live notice before paying any fee.
bullets: Check the notification’s cut-off dates; an otherwise valid qualification or age may not count if it is obtained after the specified date.
Apply for only the State/UT and vacancy options permitted in that cycle, and retain original documents for bank-stage verification.
Do not treat passing the examinations or receiving provisional allotment as automatic confirmation of final employment.

sections.heading: Exam format and scoring
paragraphs: In CRP-CSA-XV, selection began with an online Preliminary Examination followed by an online Main Examination for candidates who qualified and were shortlisted. The Preliminary Examination was 100 questions/100 marks in 60 minutes: English Language (30 questions, 30 marks, 20 minutes), Numerical Ability (35 questions, 35 marks, 20 minutes), and Reasoning Ability (35 questions, 35 marks, 20 minutes).

The CRP-CSA-XV Main Examination was 155 questions/200 marks in 120 minutes: General/Financial Awareness (40 questions, 50 marks, 20 minutes), General English (40 questions, 40 marks, 35 minutes), Reasoning Ability and Computer Aptitude (40 questions, 60 marks, 35 minutes), and Quantitative Aptitude (35 questions, 50 marks, 30 minutes). The notice stated that one-fourth of the marks assigned to a question would be deducted for each wrong answer.

The cited cycle also set out a Local Language Proficiency Test framework. Candidates should read the new notification carefully because test structure, sectional timing, scoring rules, accommodations, language provisions, and local-language requirements may be revised.
bullets: Prepare for sectional timers, not only an overall two-hour target.
Use accuracy discipline: the cited cycle’s 0.25 negative marking makes indiscriminate guessing costly.
Verify the current cycle’s cut-off, normalization, and language-test rules directly in its notification.

sections.heading: How to apply when a new CRP-CSA/clerical window opens
paragraphs: Applications are online only. In CRP-CSA-XV, applicants used the IBPS website’s CRP-CSA link, chose new registration, entered basic details, received a provisional registration number and password, completed the form, uploaded the prescribed files, paid online, and retained the application and e-receipt. IBPS’s candidate guidance says an online submission remains subject to later scrutiny and may be rejected if eligibility is not met.

Fees and dates are variable. For historical context only, the original CRP-CSA-XV notice set online registration and fee payment for 2025-08-01 to 2025-08-21 and listed ₹175 inclusive of GST for SC/ST/PwBD/ESM/DESM candidates and ₹850 inclusive of GST for all others, with bank transaction charges borne by the applicant. Those figures and dates must not be carried forward to a new cycle without confirmation.

As reviewed on 2026-07-18, the official Clerical Cadre XV page was carrying result/provisional-allotment updates rather than an advertised new CSA/clerical application window. Monitor IBPS’s CRP updates and the relevant clerical-cadre page for the next notification, then follow its exact dates and upload specifications.
bullets: Before final submission, check name, category, State/UT choice, education details, bank preferences, email address, and mobile number against documents.
Download and preserve the submitted application and fee receipt; IBPS advises candidates to retain registration communications and records.
Use only IBPS’s official website or the application portal linked from it; IBPS has published warnings concerning fraudulent websites resembling IBPS.

sections.heading: Preparation approach aligned to the official test design
paragraphs: Build preparation around the official section list and sectional time limits. For prelims, practise English, numerical ability, and reasoning in separate 20-minute blocks before combining them into full 60-minute tests. For mains, add general/financial awareness, computer-oriented reasoning, and longer quantitative and English practice under the notified sectional timers.

Use timed objective practice with an error log. Review missed questions by cause—concept gap, calculation speed, interpretation, or rushed reading—and repeat weak areas. Because CRP-CSA-XV applied a one-fourth-mark deduction for an incorrect answer, set an attempt rule that prioritises questions you can solve accurately rather than maximising raw attempts.

Treat the current official notification as the syllabus and operations checklist. Before the exam, review call-letter instructions, permitted identification, centre rules, accessibility provisions where applicable, and any official mock-test or candidate-information links published by IBPS for that cycle.
bullets: Prelims priority: speed plus accuracy in three short sections.
Mains priority: retain reasoning and quantitative accuracy while systematically covering financial awareness and English.
Take full mocks only after practising individual timed sections; then review every incorrect and skipped question.

sections.heading: What qualifying leads to—and what it does not guarantee
paragraphs: Candidates who qualify and are shortlisted after the preliminary examination take the main examination. In the latest official cycle, IBPS’s process culminated in provisional allotment to a participating bank, based on the applicable merit, preferences, vacancies, reservation guidelines, and administrative conditions described in the notification.

A provisional allotment is not the same as an appointment letter. IBPS states that its role ends after provisional allotment; the allotted bank/organisation makes the employment offer. The relevant bank may verify originals and other eligibility conditions and can reject a provisionally allotted candidate under the terms of the recruitment process.

Pay and benefits should be checked with the allotted bank rather than inferred from the IBPS exam. IBPS’s FAQ specifically directs candidates seeking pay-scale information to the bank, and the CRP-CSA-XV notification stated that allowances and perquisites are governed by the participating bank’s rules in force from time to time.
bullets: Track both the IBPS result/provisional-allotment notice and communications from the allotted bank.
Keep original degree, identity, category, disability, local-language, and other supporting documents ready for bank verification.
Compare role location, local-language conditions, compensation, joining instructions, and probation/service rules in the allotted bank’s offer documents.
